name: Rob McConnell
abstract: Robert Murray Gordon McConnell  (14 February 1935 – 1 May 2010) was a Canadian jazz trombonist, composer, and arranger. McConnell is best known for establishing and leading the big band The Boss Brass, which he directed from 1967 to 1999.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Re-Birth of the Cool

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: Rob McConnell was our soloist, from the Boss Brass, and he had one arrangement on an old record of his that I loved... I says, 'Hey I want to do ‘Every Time We Say Goodbye.’'
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Allred
about_quotes.evidence: Oh my God, I’ve only read about this guy and he’s coming to sit in with our band or coming to direct the band, and that Meredith Wilson thing and Rob McConnell was in town.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Allred interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 24, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: And all of a sudden the band started to make noise. Rob McConnell, Bob Florence, Frank Capp— and I’m sitting with the greatest thing so I figured well —
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: Sam has spent a good deal of his career in Toronto and has recorded numerous records with Rob McConnell’s Boss Brass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: I might listen to Rob McConnell or Gil Evans or Duke Ellington, people that are jazz oriented writers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: Was Brookmeyer in that? I think Brookmeyer was part — it was either Brookmeyer or Rob McConnell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024
